title: Home-place and feedback phase
summary: After the latest season and second-act framing work, lane 1 should make return payoff feel more tangible. This wave adds one visible station-or-nursery evolution pass, then one compact non-text feedback pass for departure, arrival, or route completion.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the visible home-place evolution handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The station and nursery logic is ahead of its emotional and visual payoff.
The station should look more shaped by the player's learning and returns.
The station should feel warmer and more earned, not busier.
Future feel gains should come from motion and punctuation more than more copy.

execution_order.goal: Implement non-text departure, arrival, or route-complete feedback.
execution_order.why_now: The game needs stronger tactile payoff inside existing seams.
The new feedback should improve feel without crowding the tiny screen.

guardrails: Do not add another station page or larger shell.
Prefer visual or motion payoff over new copy.
Keep the station and nursery warm, compact, and readable at 256x160.
Use existing seams and surfaces wherever possible.
Spend the home-place pass inside the current nursery cards instead of adding another text row.
For the non-text feedback pass, prefer the existing route-complete field-notice seam over a broader departure or arrival transition rewrite.
